items.text: The purpose of computing is insight, not numbers.
items.author: Richard Hamming

items.text: Computer science is no more about computers than astronomy is about telescopes.
items.author: Edsger Dijkstra

items.text: Simplicity is prerequisite for reliability.
items.author: Edsger Dijkstra

items.text: Programs must be written for people to read, and only incidentally for machines to execute.
items.author: Harold Abelson

items.text: Any fool can write code that a computer can understand. Good programmers write code that humans can understand.
items.author: Martin Fowler

items.text: Make it work, make it right, make it fast.
items.author: Kent Beck

items.text: The best error message is the one that never shows up.
items.author: Thomas Fuchs

items.text: The question of whether computers can think is like the question of whether submarines can swim.
items.author: Edsger Dijkstra

items.text: There are only two kinds of languages: the ones people complain about and the ones nobody uses.
items.author: Bjarne Stroustrup

items.text: The good thing about reinventing the wheel is that you can get a round one.
items.author: Douglas Crockford

items.text: Walking on water and developing software from a specification are easy if both are frozen.
items.author: Edward Berard

items.text: Measuring programming progress by lines of code is like measuring aircraft building progress by weight.
items.author: Bill Gates

items.text: The most disastrous thing that you can ever learn is your first programming language.
items.author: Alan Kay

items.text: Debugging is twice as hard as writing the code in the first place.
items.author: Brian Kernighan

items.text: In theory, theory and practice are the same. In practice, they are not.
items.author: Albert Einstein

items.text: The function of good software is to make the complex appear simple.
items.author: Grady Booch

items.text: First, solve the problem. Then, write the code.
items.author: John Johnson

items.text: Talk is cheap. Show me the code.
items.author: Linus Torvalds

items.text: There are two ways to write error-free programs. Only the third one works.
items.author: Alan Perlis

items.text: Give a man a program, frustrate him for a day. Teach a man to program, frustrate him for a lifetime.
items.author: Muhammad Waseem

items.text: C++ is an octopus made by nailing extra legs onto a dog.
items.author: Steve Taylor

items.text: The great thing about standards is that there are so many to choose from.
Don't worry if it doesn't work right. If everything did, you'd be out of a job.
Nothing is as permanent as a temporary solution that works.
If one programmer can do it in one week, then two programmers can do it in two weeks.
There are two types of people: those who do backups, and those who will do backups.
Hardware eventually fails. Software eventually works.
Programming is the art of adding bugs to an empty text file.
Give a man a program and he'll be frustrated for a day. Teach a man to program and he'll be frustrated for a lifetime.
Months of testing and bug fixes can save you hours of planning.
We do things not because they are easy, but because we thought they would be easy.
I don't always test my code, but when I do, it's in production.
Don't ever be so arrogant as to believe your code is correct.
